source.title: BSEE Gulf of Mexico Region Accident Investigation Report - Crane Incident

event.top_event: Deckhand Injury from Unbalanced Crane Load
actions_taken: Injured person kept onboard M/V Miss Julie
Transported to BAA 133 for medical evaluation
Helicopter transport to Christus Spohn Shoreline Hospital in Corpus Christi, Texas
Surgery performed with plate and screws installation for right leg fracture
event.summary: On 26 April 2017 at approximately 0830 hours, a Deckhand was injured while offloading a 550 gallon diesel tote tank from the M/V Miss Julie at platform BAA 105A. The Deckhand attached the crane's stringer to a pre-slung four-part wire rope sling, signaled to lift, but then moved toward the tote tank without stopping the lift when he noticed one sling leg was caught on a shackle. The tote tank lifted while unbalanced, swinging and pinning the Deckhand between the tank and starboard gunwale. The Deckhand sustained a fracture to his lower right leg and two fractured left ribs, requiring surgery.

hazards.name: Pinch Point Between Load and Structure

threats.name: Worker Entry into Hazard Area During Load Movement
Non-Standard Sling Length Contributing to Entanglement

consequences.name: Deckhand Struck by Unbalanced Tote Tank

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for Crane Operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and safe work procedures

supporting_text: The Job Safety Analysis (JSA) that was conducted for crane operations that day onto the M/V Miss Julie failed to have cooresponding dates throughout the entire JSA.
G-110(W) The JSA that was completed for the Crane operations of off loading and back loading the M/V Julie failed to have corresponding dates through out the entire JSA.

controls.name: Sling Inspection and Standardization
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to ensure proper sling configuration and prevent entanglement

supporting_text: Investigation revealed the four part sling was fabricated with approximately eleven foot legs (11'), normally the slings are fabricated with four foot legs (4'). It was surmised that this length of sling may have contributed to the leg of sling wrapping around the shackle.

G-110(W) Investigation revealed the four part sling was fabricated with approximately eleven (11') foot legs, normally the slings are fabricated with four (4') foot legs, it was surmised that this length of sling may have contributed to the leg of sling wrapping around the shackle.

controls.name: Worker Hazard Recognition and Stop-Work Authority
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control requiring worker to recognize hazards and stop unsafe operations

supporting_text: The Deckhand then noticed that one leg of the four part wire rope sling was caught on a shackle. The Deckhand proceeded to move toward the tote tank without stopping the lifting action in an attempt to free the sling.

IP approached the tote tank as it was ascending. After he gave the signal to begin the lift, he then placed his body in a pinch point between the tote tank and gunwale. IP entered the area after he had gave the signal to lift the load. IP failed to recognize the immediate hazard by not stopping the crane's actions before entering the hazardous area.
IP entered the hazard area after he had gave the signal to lift the load. IP failed to recognize the immediate hazard by not stopping the crane's actions before entering the hazardous area.

controls.name: Crane Operator Communication and Load Monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to maintain communication and monitor load stability during lift

supporting_text: The Deckhand attached the crane's stringer to a pre-slung, four part wire rope sling on the tote tank, stepped clear of the load, and then signaled to lift the load. The Deckhand then noticed that one leg of the four part wire rope sling was caught on a shackle.

controls.name: Pre-Lift Load Inspection and Verification
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to verify sling configuration and load stability before lifting

supporting_text: The Deckhand attached the crane's stringer to a pre-slung, four part wire rope sling on the tote tank, stepped clear of the load, and then signaled to lift the load. The Deckhand then noticed that one leg of the four part wire rope sling was caught on a shackle.
